toolUseResult.content:
# Restructure TopBar nav: Trending, More, Publish-as-icon

## Context

The previous cohesion pass (commits `f49567a`, `0ac4006`, `fd154f8` on
`refactor/home-cohesion-pass`) is shipped. The TopBar is now visually
correct (decision #11), but its information architecture still treats all
six entries as peers — `Skills · MCP Servers · Slash Commands · Plugins ·
Collections · Publish`. Two problems:

1. **No way back to "what's hot" from non-home pages.** Once a user has
   drilled into `/extensions` or a detail page, there's no nav shortcut to
   the curated home discovery view. We want a `Trending` entry that lands
   on the home discovery section (Recommended tab is the default there).
2. **Long tail of categories crowds the nav.** Slash Commands and Plugins
   are smaller catalogues than Skills and MCP. Demote them under a `More`
   dropdown so the primary row stays three discovery items + Collections.
3. **Publish is a write action sitting in an explore row.** Move it to the
   right group as an `Upload`-icon link next to Theme / Locale / User —
   the iconographic peers it actually belongs with.

Net result, desktop:

```
[Logo] Trending  Skills  MCP Servers  More ▾  Collections        [↥ Publish] [☼ Theme] [文/A Locale] [User]
```

Mobile is unchanged (logo + the single `Browse` link from the prior pass).

## Plan

### 1. Home anchor target — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Give the discovery `<section>` an id so `Trending` can scroll to it from
any route. The sticky 64px header would clip the heading otherwise, so
also set `scroll-margin-top` to compensate.

```diff
- <section class="mx-auto max-w-7xl px-6 py-12 sm:py-16">
+ <section
+   id="discovery"
+   class="mx-auto max-w-7xl px-6 py-12 sm:py-16 scroll-mt-16"
+ >
```

`activeTab` in `HomeDiscovery.vue` already defaults to `"official"`
(Recommended), so an anchor jump lands on the Recommended view with no
extra wiring.

### 2. TopBar restructure — `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue`

#### 2a. Split the category list

Currently one flat `CATEGORY_ITEMS` array drives the inline nav. Split it
into a *primary* set (rendered inline) and a *more* set (rendered inside
the dropdown).

```ts
type CategoryKey = "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"

const PRIMARY_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "skills", labelKey: "sidebar.skills" },
  { key: "mcp",    labelKey: "sidebar.mcpServers" },
]

const MORE_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "slash",   labelKey: "sidebar.slashCommands" },
  { key: "plugins", labelKey: "sidebar.plugins" },
]
```

#### 2b. Insert `Trending` at the head of the nav

Anchor to home discovery; locale-prefixed via `useLocalePath`. No active
state — it's a navigation shortcut, not a section indicator.

```vue
<NuxtLink
  :to="localePath({ path: '/', hash: '#discovery' })"
  class="relative px-3 py-1.5 text-(--color-ink-muted)
         transition-colors hover:text-(--color-ink)"
>
  {{ t("nav.trending") }}
</NuxtLink>
```

#### 2c. `More ▾` dropdown via existing `Popover` primitive

Use the same `Popover` + `PopoverTrigger` + `PopoverContent` pattern
already established in `app/components/layout/ThemeSwitch.vue`. No new
primitive; no `DropdownMenu` introduction.

- Trigger: a button styled to match the inline nav links exactly (same
  padding, muted ink, hover ink, bold + after-rule when *any* of the More
  items is the active route).
- Content: a 180–200px panel with a small inner padding (matches
  ThemeSwitch sizing), two NuxtLinks to `/extensions?category=slash` and
  `/extensions?category=plugins`, each with a left-rule active state
  (mirrors the sidebar treatment so the dropdown reads as a mini-sidebar).
- Caret: `ChevronDown` from `lucide-vue-next`, 14px, muted ink.

Active detection helper:

```ts
const isMoreActive = computed(() =>
  MORE_ITEMS.some(it => isCategoryActive(it.key)),
)
```

#### 2d. Demote `Publish` to a right-side icon

Remove the `Publish` link from the inline nav. Add it on the right side,
**before** `ThemeSwitch`, sized and styled to match the existing utility
buttons (32px circle, transparent border, hover bg, focus ring) — see
ThemeSwitch trigger lines 37–42 as the reference shape.

```vue
<NuxtLink
  :to="localePath('/publish')"
  class="hidden md:inline-flex size-8 rounded-full items-center justify-center
         border border-transparent text-(--color-ink-muted)
         hover:bg-(--color-sidebar) hover:text-(--color-ink)
         focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2
         focus-visible:ring-(--color-accent)"
  :aria-label="t('nav.publish')"
  :title="t('nav.publish')"
  active-class="text-(--color-ink)"
>
  <Upload :size="16" aria-hidden="true" />
</NuxtLink>
```

`active-class` flips the icon to full ink when on `/publish`. No bottom
rule — the right-side utilities use color-only active states (matches
ThemeSwitch's `data-[state=open]:border-(--color-border)` pattern, though
we don't need an open state for a plain link).

Imports to add: `import { ChevronDown, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"`.

#### 2e. Mobile

The existing `md:hidden` Browse link stays. The new Trending / More /
Collections nav and the new Publish icon all live inside the existing
`hidden md:flex` nav (or are explicitly `hidden md:inline-flex` in the
Publish icon's case). Mobile bar remains: `Logo · Browse · Theme · Locale ·
User` — unchanged.

### 3. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

Add two new keys under `nav`. The slash/plugins menu items reuse the
existing `sidebar.slashCommands` and `sidebar.plugins` translations
(already present and correct in both locales).

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "Account menu",
     "comingSoon": "Coming soon",
-    "browse": "Browse"
+    "browse": "Browse",
+    "trending": "Trending",
+    "more": "More"
   },
```

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "账号菜单",
     "comingSoon": "敬请期待",
-    "browse": "浏览"
+    "browse": "浏览",
+    "trending": "热榜",
+    "more": "更多"
   },
```

### 4. Tests

- No existing `TopBar.nuxt.test.ts`. Skip — visual changes covered by PR
  review.
- `app/components/layout/{Sidebar,LocaleSwitch,ThemeSwitch}.nuxt.test.ts`
  are unaffected; they don't import `TopBar`.

## Critical files

- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — primary work: split items, add
  Trending link, add More popover, move Publish to right group, add
  `Upload` + `ChevronDown` imports.
- `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue` — single line: add `id` and
  `scroll-mt-16` on the section.
- `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — `nav.trending` +
  `nav.more` only.

## Reused primitives (not changed)

- `Popover`, `PopoverTrigger`, `PopoverContent` from `app/components/ui/`
  (same trio used by `ThemeSwitch.vue` and `SaveToCollectionPopover.vue`).
- `useLocalePath`, `useI18n`, `useRoute` (all already used in current
  `TopBar.vue`).
- Lucide icons via `lucide-vue-next` (already a project dep).

## Out of scope (intentionally)

- No new `DropdownMenu` shadcn-vue component (Popover suffices).
- No changes to the `Sidebar`, `HomeHero`, `HomeTabs`, `HomeTagRail`, or
  any extension/detail components.
- No mobile sheet/hamburger redesign.
- No new routes — `Trending` is just `/#discovery`.

## Verification

1. `bun run dev`. From `/en` (any non-home page, e.g. `/en/extensions`):
   - Click `Trending` — URL changes to `/en/#discovery`, page scrolls to
     the discovery section with the heading visible (not clipped under
     the sticky header).
   - Repeat from `/en/cli` and `/en/collections` to confirm cross-route
     behavior.
2. Click `More ▾`:
   - Popover opens below the trigger with two items: Slash Commands and
     Plugins, both linking to `/extensions?category=…`.
   - Navigating to either category closes the popover and lands on the
     filtered grid.
   - When already on a More category, the `More` trigger shows the same
     bold + bottom-rule active treatment as the primary nav items.
3. Hover and click the `Upload` icon in the right group:
   - Tooltip / `aria-label` reads "Publish" (zh: "发布").
   - Click navigates to `/{locale}/publish`. While on `/publish`, the icon
     renders in full ink (otherwise muted).
4. Resize <768px:
   - Trending / More / Collections / Publish-icon all disappear.
   - The `Browse` link from the previous pass remains visible.
5. Theme toggle through Ivory / Dark / Mono — Popover + icon read
   correctly in all three.
6. Keyboard: tab through TopBar — order is Logo → Browse (mobile only) →
   Trending → Skills → MCP Servers → More trigger → Collections →
   Publish icon → Theme → Locale → User. Popover opens on Enter/Space and
   closes on Escape (Reka UI default).
7. Repeat the same checks from `/zh` to confirm copy: 热榜 / 更多 / 发布.
8. `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` all green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

One feature branch (`refactor/topbar-restructure` or similar), expect ~2
commits:

- `feat(topbar): add Trending shortcut and collapse Slash/Plugins under More`
- `refactor(topbar): demote Publish to a right-side Upload icon`

Could also be a single `refactor(topbar): restructure nav into primary/
more/publish-icon` if the diff is tight. No agent-attribution trailer per
project convention.

```ts
  { key: "slash",   labelKey: "sidebar.slashCommands" },
  { key: "plugins", labelKey: "sidebar.plugins" },
]
```

#### 2b. Insert `Trending` at the head of the nav

Anchor to home discovery; locale-prefixed via `useLocalePath`. No active
state — it's a navigation shortcut, not a section indicator.

```vue
<NuxtLink
  :to="localePath({ path: '/', hash: '#discovery' })"
  class="relative px-3 py-1.5 text-(--color-ink-muted)
         transition-colors hover:text-(--color-ink)"
>
  {{ t("nav.trending") }}
</NuxtLink>
```

#### 2c. `More ▾` dropdown via existing `Popover` primitive

Use the same `Popover` + `PopoverTrigger` + `PopoverContent` pattern
already established in `app/components/layout/ThemeSwitch.vue`. No new
primitive; no `DropdownMenu` introduction.

- Trigger: a button styled to match the inline nav links exactly (same
  padding, muted ink, hover ink, bold + after-rule when *any* of the More
  items is the active route).
- Content: a 180–200px panel with a small inner padding (matches
  ThemeSwitch sizing), two NuxtLinks to `/extensions?category=slash` and
  `/extensions?category=plugins`, each with a left-rule active state
  (mirrors the sidebar treatment so the dropdown reads as a mini-sidebar).
- Caret: `ChevronDown` from `lucide-vue-next`, 14px, muted ink.

Active detection helper:

```ts
const isMoreActive = computed(() =>
  MORE_ITEMS.some(it => isCategoryActive(it.key)),
)
```

#### 2d. Demote `Publish` to a right-side icon

Remove the `Publish` link from the inline nav. Add it on the right side,
**before** `ThemeSwitch`, sized and styled to match the existing utility
buttons (32px circle, transparent border, hover bg, focus ring) — see
ThemeSwitch trigger lines 37–42 as the reference shape.

```vue

  :to="localePath('/publish')"
  class="hidden md:inline-flex size-8 rounded-full items-center justify-center
         border border-transparent text-(--color-ink-muted)
         hover:bg-(--color-sidebar) hover:text-(--color-ink)
         focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2
         focus-visible:ring-(--color-accent)"
  :aria-label="t('nav.publish')"
  :title="t('nav.publish')"
  active-class="text-(--color-ink)"
>
  <Upload :size="16" aria-hidden="true" />
</NuxtLink>
```

`active-class` flips the icon to full ink when on `/publish`. No bottom
rule — the right-side utilities use color-only active states (matches
ThemeSwitch's `data-[state=open]:border-(--color-border)` pattern, though
we don't need an open state for a plain link).

Imports to add: `import { ChevronDown, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"`.

#### 2e. Mobile

The existing `md:hidden` Browse link stays. The new Trending / More /
Collections nav and the new Publish icon all live inside the existing
`hidden md:flex` nav (or are explicitly `hidden md:inline-flex` in the
Publish icon's case). Mobile bar remains: `Logo · Browse · Theme · Locale ·
User` — unchanged.

### 3. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

Add two new keys under `nav`. The slash/plugins menu items reuse the
existing `sidebar.slashCommands` and `sidebar.plugins` translations
(already present and correct in both locales).

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "Account menu",
     "comingSoon": "Coming soon",
-    "browse": "Browse"
+    "browse": "Browse",
+    "trending": "Trending",
+    "more": "More"
   },
```

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "账号菜单",
     "comingSoon": "敬请期待",
-    "browse": "浏览"
+    "browse": "浏览",
+    "trending": "热榜",
+    "more": "更多"
   },
```

### 4. Tests

- No existing `TopBar.nuxt.test.ts`. Skip — visual changes covered by PR
  review.
- `app/components/layout/{Sidebar,LocaleSwitch,ThemeSwitch}.nuxt.test.ts`
  are unaffected; they don't import `TopBar`.

## Critical files

- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — primary work: split items, add
  Trending link, add More popover, move Publish to right group, add
  `Upload` + `ChevronDown` imports.
- `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue` — single line: add `id` and
  `scroll-mt-16` on the section.
- `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — `nav.trending` +
  `nav.more` only.

## Reused primitives (not changed)

- `Popover`, `PopoverTrigger`, `PopoverContent` from `app/components/ui/`
  (same trio used by `ThemeSwitch.vue` and `SaveToCollectionPopover.vue`).
- `useLocalePath`, `useI18n`, `useRoute` (all already used in current
  `TopBar.vue`).
- Lucide icons via `lucide-vue-next` (already a project dep).

## Out of scope (intentionally)

- No new `DropdownMenu` shadcn-vue component (Popover suffices).
- No changes to the `Sidebar`, `HomeHero`, `HomeTabs`, `HomeTagRail`, or
  any extension/detail components.
- No mobile sheet/hamburger redesign.
- No new routes — `Trending` is just `/#discovery`.

## Verification

1. `bun run dev`. From `/en` (any non-home page, e.g. `/en/extensions`):
   - Click `Trending` — URL changes to `/en/#discovery`, page scrolls to
     the discovery section with the heading visible (not clipped under
     the sticky header).
   - Repeat from `/en/cli` and `/en/collections` to confirm cross-route
     behavior.
2. Click `More ▾`:
   - Popover opens below the trigger with two items: Slash Commands and
     Plugins, both linking to `/extensions?category=…`.
   - Navigating to either category closes the popover and lands on the
     filtered grid.
   - When already on a More category, the `More` trigger shows the same
     bold + bottom-rule active treatment as the primary nav items.
3. Hover and click the `Upload` icon in the right group:
   - Tooltip / `aria-label` reads "Publish" (zh: "发布").
   - Click navigates to `/{locale}/publish`. While on `/publish`, the icon
     renders in full ink (otherwise muted).
4. Resize <768px:
   - Trending / More / Collections / Publish-icon all disappear.
   - The `Browse` link from the previous pass remains visible.
5. Theme toggle through Ivory / Dark / Mono — Popover + icon read
   correctly in all three.
6. Keyboard: tab through TopBar — order is Logo → Browse (mobile only) →
   Trending → Skills → MCP Servers → More trigger → Collections →
   Publish icon → Theme → Locale → User. Popover opens on Enter/Space and
   closes on Escape (Reka UI default).
7. Repeat the same checks from `/zh` to confirm copy: 热榜 / 更多 / 发布.
8. `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` all green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

One feature branch (`refactor/topbar-restructure` or similar), expect ~2
commits:

- `feat(topbar): add Trending shortcut and collapse Slash/Plugins under More`
- `refactor(topbar): demote Publish to a right-side Upload icon`

Could also be a single `refactor(topbar): restructure nav into primary/
more/publish-icon` if the diff is tight. No agent-attribution trailer per
project convention.

# Restructure TopBar nav: Trending, More, Publish-as-icon

## Context

The previous cohesion pass (commits `f49567a`, `0ac4006`, `fd154f8` on
`refactor/home-cohesion-pass`) is shipped. The TopBar is now visually
correct (decision #11), but its information architecture still treats all
six entries as peers — `Skills · MCP Servers · Slash Commands · Plugins ·
Collections · Publish`. Two problems:

1. **No way back to "what's hot" from non-home pages.** Once a user has
   drilled into `/extensions` or a detail page, there's no nav shortcut to
   the curated home discovery view. We want a `Trending` entry that lands
   on the home discovery section (Recommended tab is the default there).
2. **Long tail of categories crowds the nav.** Slash Commands and Plugins
   are smaller catalogues than Skills and MCP. Demote them under a `More`
   dropdown so the primary row stays three discovery items + Collections.
3. **Publish is a write action sitting in an explore row.** Move it to the
   right group as an `Upload`-icon link next to Theme / Locale / User —
   the iconographic peers it actually belongs with.

Net result, desktop:

```
[Logo] Trending  Skills  MCP Servers  More ▾  Collections        [↥ Publish] [☼ Theme] [文/A Locale] [User]
```

Mobile is unchanged (logo + the single `Browse` link from the prior pass).

### 1. Home anchor target — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Give the discovery `<section>` an id so `Trending` can scroll to it from

```diff
+ <section

+ >
```

`activeTab` in `HomeDiscovery.vue` already defaults to `"official"`
(Recommended), so an anchor jump lands on the Recommended view with no

     bold + bottom-rule active treatment as the primary nav items.
3. Hover and click the `Upload` icon in the right group:
   - Tooltip / `aria-label` reads "Publish" (zh: "发布").
   - Click navigates to `/{locale}/publish`. While on `/publish`, the icon
     renders in full ink (otherwise muted).
4. Resize <768px:
   - Trending / More / Collections / Publish-icon all disappear.
   - The `Browse` link from the previous pass remains visible.
5. Theme toggle through Ivory / Dark / Mono — Popover + icon read
   correctly in all three.
6. Keyboard: tab through TopBar — order is Logo → Browse (mobile only) →
   Trending → Skills → MCP Servers → More trigger → Collections →
   Publish icon → Theme → Locale → User. Popover opens on Enter/Space and
   closes on Escape (Reka UI default).
7. Repeat the same checks from `/zh` to confirm copy: 热榜 / 更多 / 发布.
8. `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` all green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

One feature branch (`refactor/topbar-restructure` or similar), expect ~2
commits:

- `feat(topbar): add Trending shortcut and collapse Slash/Plugins under More`
- `refactor(topbar): demote Publish to a right-side Upload icon`

Could also be a single `refactor(topbar): restructure nav into primary/
more/publish-icon` if the diff is tight. No agent-attribution trailer per
project convention.

  style left-rule active indicator.

  next to ThemeSwitch — matching the iconographic peer group it actually
  belongs with. Color-only active state (full ink on /publish), no

  Logo  Trending  Skills  MCP Servers  More ▾  Collections   ↥ Publish ☼ 文 ◯

Mobile is unchanged (logo + Browse link only).
EOF
)"

content.text:
Branch `refactor/home-cohesion-pass` now has 4 commits — the prior cohesion pass (3) plus the new TopBar restructure (1).

**TopBar restructure summary:**
- **Trending** at the head of the nav → `/{locale}/#discovery` (lands on the default Recommended tab in `HomeDiscovery`, with `scroll-mt-16` so the sticky header doesn't clip it).
- **Slash Commands + Plugins** collapsed into a **More ▾** dropdown using the existing `Popover` primitive (no new shadcn-vue component). Active state on the trigger when any of its items matches the current category; sidebar-style left-rule on the items themselves.
- **Publish** moved from an inline explore link to a right-side **Upload** icon next to ThemeSwitch — color-only active state to match its iconographic peers.
- New i18n keys: `nav.trending` (Trending / 热榜), `nav.more` (More / 更多).

Mobile is untouched: logo + Browse link only.

`bun run lint`, `bun run typecheck`, `bun run test` (242/242) all green. Nothing pushed.

message.content: is it better to put more at the last? what do you think?

content.text: Yes — convention is on your side: residual "More" menus almost always trail (iOS tab bars, Office ribbon, GitHub's overflow nav). The main tradeoff is that putting it after Collections breaks the implicit "all categories sit together" grouping, but I think the convention win outweighs that — `Trending | Skills | MCP Servers | Collections | More ▾` reads as "primary nav, plus overflow" which is what we actually mean. Want me to reorder it?

file.content:
# Restructure TopBar nav: Trending, More, Publish-as-icon

## Context

The previous cohesion pass (commits `f49567a`, `0ac4006`, `fd154f8` on
`refactor/home-cohesion-pass`) is shipped. The TopBar is now visually
correct (decision #11), but its information architecture still treats all
six entries as peers — `Skills · MCP Servers · Slash Commands · Plugins ·
Collections · Publish`. Two problems:

1. **No way back to "what's hot" from non-home pages.** Once a user has
   drilled into `/extensions` or a detail page, there's no nav shortcut to
   the curated home discovery view. We want a `Trending` entry that lands
   on the home discovery section (Recommended tab is the default there).
2. **Long tail of categories crowds the nav.** Slash Commands and Plugins

content.text:
I have a clean read on the trade-offs. One thing I want to pin down before writing the plan — "3 lines" is viewport-dependent (the grid is `auto-fill` 320px-min), so it lands differently at common widths:

| count | desktop (3 col) | tablet (2 col) | mobile (1 col) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 9 | 3 lines | 4.5 ← half row | 9 |
| 12 (current) | 4 lines | 6 | 12 |
| 18 | 6 lines | 9 | 18 |
| 24 | 8 lines | 12 | 24 |

Since you said "show *more*", 9 is out (fewer than today). Picking one that gives full rows on both 3-col and 2-col widths matters.

questions.question: How many cards should each discovery tab show on the home?

options.label: 18 (Recommended)
options.description: 6 lines on a 3-col desktop, 9 lines on 2-col tablet. Visibly 'more' than the current 12 without overwhelming, and falls on clean row boundaries everywhere.
8 lines on desktop, 12 on tablet. Maxes out the API page (PAGE_SIZE = 24), so a single fetch fills the section. Heavier scroll cost on mobile.

options.label: Keep 12
options.description: Status quo. Maybe the 'show more' intent is the heading-wording fix alone.

content.text:
Now I have a clear picture. The "1 line, 3 cards" you're seeing on Recommended is bounded by the **seed data**, not the slice limit (12) or the API page size (24). Only ~3 skills are flagged `badge: "official"` in the catalog seed, and `Recommended` filters by exactly that flag (`shared/search/query.ts:58-60`). Popular and New tabs aren't filtered and would already show many more — that's worth verifying.

Three ways to fix Recommended; each has a different feel.

questions.question: Which approach should the plan take for the sparse Recommended tab?

options.label: Flag more skills as official (Recommended)
options.description: Editorial: bump 'badge: "official"' on ~12-18 high-quality skills in shared/data/catalog.ts. Keeps Recommended truly curated. I can pick a sensible spread across categories and you review the diff.

options.label: Top-up fallback when sparse
options.description: Engineering: keep badge='official' as the primary signal, but if fewer than N items match, append top-by-downloads skills to fill the row. Recommended always feels populated, but the semantics blur with Popular.

options.label: Redefine Recommended
options.description: Drop the strict badge filter. Recommended becomes 'top picks by downloads + recency' — effectively a blended editorial signal. Closer to what 'Trending' means on most marketplaces.

questions.question: Heading wording — fix it the same way?

options.label: '{count} skills for your team.' (Recommended)
options.description: Match what's actually shown. Also filter the total fetch to category=skills so the number is consistent with the grid.

options.label: Keep 'extensions'
options.description: Use the cross-category total as a 'whole marketplace size' headline; accept the small mismatch with the skills-only grid.

file.content:
// Catalog data for the idempotent seed. Each entry becomes one row in the
// `extensions` table with id = `cat-${slug}`. `scripts/seed-catalog.ts` upserts
// these on every deploy via the `vercel-build` chain, so editing this file
// changes prod content on the next deploy.
//
// Slugs must NOT collide with the destructive demo seed (`scripts/seed.ts`),
// which uses `web-search-pro`, `code-interpreter`, `image-vision`,
// `arch-reviewer`, `data-analyst`, `test-generator`, `github-mcp`,
// `postgres-mcp`, `slack-mcp`, `k8s-mcp`, `summarize`, `translate`, `explain`,
// `notion-sync`, `calendar-agent`, `mqtt-bridge`. Slugs here are distinct so
// both seeds can coexist in dev without unique-constraint conflicts.

import type { IconColor } from "./icon-colors"

export interface CatalogEntry {
  slug: string
  category: "skills" | "slash" | "plugins"
  scope: "personal" | "org" | "enterprise"
  funcCat: "workTask" | "business" | "tools"
  subCat: string
  l2: string
  name: string
  nameZh: string
  tagline: string
  taglineZh: string
  description: string
  descriptionZh: string
  iconEmoji: string
  iconColor: IconColor
  tags: string[]
  badge?: "official" | "popular" | "new"
  downloadsCount: number
  starsAvg: string
}

// Color rotation: indigo / amber / emerald / rose / slate (from icon-colors.ts).
// No reuse of `--color-accent` — per CLAUDE.md §11, accent is reserved for CTAs.

const SKILLS: CatalogEntry[] = [
  // ── workTask · systemDesign ─────────────────────────────────────────────
  {
    slug: "user-story-writer",
    category: "skills",
    scope: "personal",
    funcCat: "workTask",
    subCat: "systemDesign",
    l2: "reqAnalysis",
    name: "User Story Writer",
    nameZh: "用户故事生成器",
    tagline: "Turn product asks into INVEST stories with acceptance criteria.",
    taglineZh: "将产品需求转化为带验收标准的 INVEST 用户故事。",
    description: "Drafts well-formed user stories from a product brief: role, goal, motivation, plus Given/When/Then acceptance criteria. Catches missing edge cases by walking through the INVEST checklist.",
    descriptionZh: "根据产品简介起草规范的用户故事：角色、目标、动机，以及 Given/When/Then 验收标准。通过 INVEST 清单发现遗漏的边界情况。",
    iconEmoji: "📝",
    iconColor: "indigo",
    tags: ["docs", "architecture", "stable"],
    downloadsCount: 41200,
    starsAvg: "4.6",
  },
  {

    descriptionZh: "包含 5 Whys、Job Story、事前剖析等引导提问模板的提示库，对干系人访谈进行追问以揭示未言明的约束、成功指标和失败模式。",
    iconEmoji: "🔍",
    iconColor: "amber",
    tags: ["search", "review", "stable"],
    downloadsCount: 18700,

input.content:
# Discovery section: skills-aware heading + populate Recommended tab

## Context

The home discovery section currently shows three tabs (Recommended /
Popular / New), all of which hard-code `category: "skills"` in their
fetch — so the grid is skills-only. Two mismatches result:

1. **Heading lies.** The headline reads `{count} extensions for your
   team.`, but the count is a *cross-category* total (skills + MCP +
   slash + plugins) while the grid below only renders skills. The user
   asked for the heading to say "skills" — and we should also filter the
   `totalData` fetch to `category=skills` so the number matches reality.
2. **Recommended is sparse.** It shows a single row of 3 cards. The
   filter is `eq(extensions.badge, "official")` (`shared/search/query.ts:58-60`),
   and only **2** catalog entries currently have `badge: "official"`:
   `sql-tuner` and `aws-iam-policy-helper`. The slice limit (12) and API
   page size (24) are not the bottleneck — content is. The other tabs
   (Popular, New) are unfiltered so they already fill several rows.

User decision (this session): flag more catalog skills as official so
Recommended fills out naturally, rather than redefining the tab or
bolting on a fallback.

## Plan

### 1. Heading copy + scope — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Filter `totalData` to skills, and update the i18n strings. The grid-data
fetch already filters to skills, so we just align the headline.

```diff
 const { data: totalData } = await useFetch("/api/internal/extensions", {
   key: "home-discovery-total",
+  query: { category: "skills" },
   default: () => ({
     items: [] as ExtensionListItem[],
     total: 0,
     filters: {},
   }),
 })
```

### 2. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} extensions for your team.",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI extensions for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} skills for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI skills for your team.",
```

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个扩展，为你的团队而备。",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 扩展，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个技能，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 技能，为你的团队而备。",
```

### 3. Promote 11 catalog skills to `badge: "official"` — `shared/data/catalog.ts`

Editorial picks chosen for: ≥4.5★, healthy downloads, and a balanced
spread across `funcCat` × `subCat` cells so Recommended reads as a real
curation rather than "popular sorted differently". Skips entries already
flagged `popular` (those represent a separate badge axis).

End state: **13 official skills** (current 2 + 11 new), enough to fill
~4 rows on a 3-col desktop grid (12 cards on screen) with one spare.

| slug | funcCat / subCat | dl | ★ | reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sql-tuner` *(keep)* | workTask / softDev | 142.5k | 4.9 | already official |
| `aws-iam-policy-helper` *(keep)* | business / cloud | 102.3k | 4.8 | already official |
| `api-spec-helper` | workTask / systemDesign | 52.8k | 4.6 | OpenAPI/REST helper, broadly useful |
| `dockerfile-linter` | workTask / softDev | 89.3k | 4.7 | container hygiene, high signal |
| `react-hook-genie` | workTask / softDev | 72.4k | 4.7 | frontend mainline |
| `vitest-case-writer` | workTask / testing | 56.7k | 4.7 | top of testing subcat |
| `postman-helper` | business / network | 47.2k | 4.5 | best-of-class for network |
| `k8s-manifest-gen` | business / cloud | 78.5k | 4.7 | second cloud pick alongside IAM |
| `readme-generator` | tools / docs | 76.3k | 4.7 | universal team doc |
| `regex-builder` | tools / data | 51.2k | 4.7 | high-utility data tool |
| `conventional-commit-helper` | tools / vcs | 89.1k | 4.7 | strong vcs default |
| `github-actions-helper` | tools / vcs | 61.8k | 4.7 | second vcs pick |
| `test-this` | workTask / testing | 56.2k | 4.7 | filler-zone but quality-fit |

Spread: workTask 6 / business 3 / tools 4. Sub-balance: softDev 3,
testing 2, vcs 2, cloud 2, systemDesign 1, network 1, docs 1, data 1.

Edit pattern per entry — insert `badge: "official",` immediately before
`downloadsCount:`, matching the existing 2 entries' shape (see lines
231-233 and 639-641).

The seed script `scripts/seed-catalog.ts` upserts these entries on every
`vercel-build`, so the database picks up the badges on the next deploy.
For local dev: re-run `bun run scripts/seed-catalog.ts` (or hit
`db:seed:catalog` if a script alias exists).

### 4. (Out of scope this pass)

- No change to the slice limit (`items.slice(0, 12)`) — 12 is fine; the
  earlier "show more" perception came from sparse data, not the cap.
- No tab semantic changes.
- No new component or layout work.
- No changes to Popular / New tabs (already populated).

## Critical files

- `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue` — add `query: { category: "skills" }`
  to the `home-discovery-total` `useFetch`.
- `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — `discoveryHeading` +
  `discoveryHeadingEmpty` copy.
- `shared/data/catalog.ts` — add `badge: "official"` to 11 entries
  (slugs listed above).

## Reused mechanisms

- `badge: "official"` field on `CatalogEntry` (already in the schema and
  consumed by the seed at `scripts/seed-catalog.ts`).
- `filter === "official"` predicate in `shared/search/query.ts:58-60`,
  unchanged.
- `useFetch` keying in `HomeDiscovery.vue` — only the query payload of
  `home-discovery-total` changes; the cache key stays stable.

## Verification

1. Re-seed the local DB so the new badges apply:

   ```sh
   bun run scripts/seed-catalog.ts
   ```

2. `bun run dev`. Open `/en`:
   - Discovery heading reads `13 skills for your team.` (or whatever the
     skills total is after seeding — should match the count of catalog
     skills, not the cross-category total).
   - **Recommended** tab fills ~4 rows on a 3-col desktop (≈13 cards).
     Scan for category spread — should see a mix across systemDesign,
     softDev, testing, network, cloud, docs, data, vcs.
   - **Popular** + **New** tabs unchanged in behavior.
3. Repeat on `/zh` — heading should read "13 个技能，为你的团队而备。"
4. Hit the API directly:
   `GET /api/internal/extensions?category=skills&filter=official` —
   `total` should be 13.
5. Lint + typecheck + tests: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`.
   None of the changes touch test fixtures or source TypeScript types, so
   the 242-test suite should stay green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

Two commits on the existing `refactor/home-cohesion-pass` branch:

- `feat(seed): promote 11 catalog skills to badge=official`
- `refactor(home): heading says "skills"; total filtered to skills-only`

No agent-attribution trailer.

| `react-hook-genie` | workTask / softDev | 72.4k | 4.7 | frontend mainline |
| `vitest-case-writer` | workTask / testing | 56.7k | 4.7 | top of testing subcat |
| `postman-helper` | business / network | 47.2k | 4.5 | best-of-class for network |
| `k8s-manifest-gen` | business / cloud | 78.5k | 4.7 | second cloud pick alongside IAM |
| `readme-generator` | tools / docs | 76.3k | 4.7 | universal team doc |
| `regex-builder` | tools / data | 51.2k | 4.7 | high-utility data tool |
| `conventional-commit-helper` | tools / vcs | 89.1k | 4.7 | strong vcs default |
| `github-actions-helper` | tools / vcs | 61.8k | 4.7 | second vcs pick |
| `test-this` | workTask / testing | 56.2k | 4.7 | filler-zone but quality-fit |

Spread: workTask 6 / business 3 / tools 4. Sub-balance: softDev 3,
testing 2, vcs 2, cloud 2, systemDesign 1, network 1, docs 1, data 1.

Edit pattern per entry — insert `badge: "official",` immediately before
`downloadsCount:`, matching the existing 2 entries' shape (see lines
231-233 and 639-641).

The seed script `scripts/seed-catalog.ts` upserts these entries on every
`vercel-build`, so the database picks up the badges on the next deploy.
For local dev: re-run `bun run scripts/seed-catalog.ts` (or hit
`db:seed:catalog` if a script alias exists).

### 4. (Out of scope this pass)

- No change to the slice limit (`items.slice(0, 12)`) — 12 is fine; the
  earlier "show more" perception came from sparse data, not the cap.
- No tab semantic changes.
- No new component or layout work.
- No changes to Popular / New tabs (already populated).

## Critical files

- `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue` — add `query: { category: "skills" }`
  to the `home-discovery-total` `useFetch`.
- `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — `discoveryHeading` +
  `discoveryHeadingEmpty` copy.
- `shared/data/catalog.ts` — add `badge: "official"` to 11 entries
  (slugs listed above).

## Reused mechanisms

- `badge: "official"` field on `CatalogEntry` (already in the schema and
  consumed by the seed at `scripts/seed-catalog.ts`).
- `filter === "official"` predicate in `shared/search/query.ts:58-60`,
  unchanged.
- `useFetch` keying in `HomeDiscovery.vue` — only the query payload of
  `home-discovery-total` changes; the cache key stays stable.

## Verification

1. Re-seed the local DB so the new badges apply:

   ```sh
   bun run scripts/seed-catalog.ts
   ```

2. `bun run dev`. Open `/en`:
   - Discovery heading reads `13 skills for your team.` (or whatever the
     skills total is after seeding — should match the count of catalog
     skills, not the cross-category total).
   - **Recommended** tab fills ~4 rows on a 3-col desktop (≈13 cards).
     Scan for category spread — should see a mix across systemDesign,
     softDev, testing, network, cloud, docs, data, vcs.
   - **Popular** + **New** tabs unchanged in behavior.
3. Repeat on `/zh` — heading should read "13 个技能，为你的团队而备。"
4. Hit the API directly:
   `GET /api/internal/extensions?category=skills&filter=official` —
   `total` should be 13.
5. Lint + typecheck + tests: `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test`.
   None of the changes touch test fixtures or source TypeScript types, so
   the 242-test suite should stay green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

Two commits on the existing `refactor/home-cohesion-pass` branch:

- `feat(seed): promote 11 catalog skills to badge=official`
- `refactor(home): heading says "skills"; total filtered to skills-only`

No agent-attribution trailer.

# Restructure TopBar nav: Trending, More, Publish-as-icon

## Context

The previous cohesion pass (commits `f49567a`, `0ac4006`, `fd154f8` on
`refactor/home-cohesion-pass`) is shipped. The TopBar is now visually
correct (decision #11), but its information architecture still treats all
six entries as peers — `Skills · MCP Servers · Slash Commands · Plugins ·
Collections · Publish`. Two problems:

1. **No way back to "what's hot" from non-home pages.** Once a user has
   drilled into `/extensions` or a detail page, there's no nav shortcut to
   the curated home discovery view. We want a `Trending` entry that lands
   on the home discovery section (Recommended tab is the default there).
2. **Long tail of categories crowds the nav.** Slash Commands and Plugins
   are smaller catalogues than Skills and MCP. Demote them under a `More`
   dropdown so the primary row stays three discovery items + Collections.
3. **Publish is a write action sitting in an explore row.** Move it to the
   right group as an `Upload`-icon link next to Theme / Locale / User —
   the iconographic peers it actually belongs with.

Net result, desktop:

```
[Logo] Trending  Skills  MCP Servers  More ▾  Collections        [↥ Publish] [☼ Theme] [文/A Locale] [User]
```

Mobile is unchanged (logo + the single `Browse` link from the prior pass).

## Plan

### 1. Home anchor target — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Give the discovery `<section>` an id so `Trending` can scroll to it from
any route. The sticky 64px header would clip the heading otherwise, so
also set `scroll-margin-top` to compensate.

```diff
- <section class="mx-auto max-w-7xl px-6 py-12 sm:py-16">
+ <section
+   id="discovery"
+   class="mx-auto max-w-7xl px-6 py-12 sm:py-16 scroll-mt-16"
+ >
```

`activeTab` in `HomeDiscovery.vue` already defaults to `"official"`
(Recommended), so an anchor jump lands on the Recommended view with no
extra wiring.

### 2. TopBar restructure — `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue`

#### 2a. Split the category list

Currently one flat `CATEGORY_ITEMS` array drives the inline nav. Split it
into a *primary* set (rendered inline) and a *more* set (rendered inside
the dropdown).

```ts
type CategoryKey = "skills" | "mcp" | "slash" | "plugins"

const PRIMARY_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "skills", labelKey: "sidebar.skills" },
  { key: "mcp",    labelKey: "sidebar.mcpServers" },
]

const MORE_ITEMS: { key: CategoryKey; labelKey: string }[] = [
  { key: "slash",   labelKey: "sidebar.slashCommands" },
  { key: "plugins", labelKey: "sidebar.plugins" },
]
```

#### 2b. Insert `Trending` at the head of the nav

Anchor to home discovery; locale-prefixed via `useLocalePath`. No active
state — it's a navigation shortcut, not a section indicator.

```vue
<NuxtLink
  :to="localePath({ path: '/', hash: '#discovery' })"
  class="relative px-3 py-1.5 text-(--color-ink-muted)
         transition-colors hover:text-(--color-ink)"
>
  {{ t("nav.trending") }}
</NuxtLink>
```

#### 2c. `More ▾` dropdown via existing `Popover` primitive

Use the same `Popover` + `PopoverTrigger` + `PopoverContent` pattern
already established in `app/components/layout/ThemeSwitch.vue`. No new
primitive; no `DropdownMenu` introduction.

- Trigger: a button styled to match the inline nav links exactly (same
  padding, muted ink, hover ink, bold + after-rule when *any* of the More
  items is the active route).
- Content: a 180–200px panel with a small inner padding (matches
  ThemeSwitch sizing), two NuxtLinks to `/extensions?category=slash` and
  `/extensions?category=plugins`, each with a left-rule active state
  (mirrors the sidebar treatment so the dropdown reads as a mini-sidebar).
- Caret: `ChevronDown` from `lucide-vue-next`, 14px, muted ink.

Active detection helper:

```ts
const isMoreActive = computed(() =>
  MORE_ITEMS.some(it => isCategoryActive(it.key)),
)
```

#### 2d. Demote `Publish` to a right-side icon

Remove the `Publish` link from the inline nav. Add it on the right side,
**before** `ThemeSwitch`, sized and styled to match the existing utility
buttons (32px circle, transparent border, hover bg, focus ring) — see
ThemeSwitch trigger lines 37–42 as the reference shape.

```vue
<NuxtLink
  :to="localePath('/publish')"
  class="hidden md:inline-flex size-8 rounded-full items-center justify-center
         border border-transparent text-(--color-ink-muted)
         hover:bg-(--color-sidebar) hover:text-(--color-ink)
         focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2
         focus-visible:ring-(--color-accent)"
  :aria-label="t('nav.publish')"
  :title="t('nav.publish')"
  active-class="text-(--color-ink)"
>
  <Upload :size="16" aria-hidden="true" />
</NuxtLink>
```

`active-class` flips the icon to full ink when on `/publish`. No bottom
rule — the right-side utilities use color-only active states (matches
ThemeSwitch's `data-[state=open]:border-(--color-border)` pattern, though
we don't need an open state for a plain link).

Imports to add: `import { ChevronDown, Upload } from "lucide-vue-next"`.

#### 2e. Mobile

The existing `md:hidden` Browse link stays. The new Trending / More /
Collections nav and the new Publish icon all live inside the existing
`hidden md:flex` nav (or are explicitly `hidden md:inline-flex` in the
Publish icon's case). Mobile bar remains: `Logo · Browse · Theme · Locale ·
User` — unchanged.

### 3. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

Add two new keys under `nav`. The slash/plugins menu items reuse the
existing `sidebar.slashCommands` and `sidebar.plugins` translations
(already present and correct in both locales).

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "Account menu",
     "comingSoon": "Coming soon",
-    "browse": "Browse"
+    "browse": "Browse",
+    "trending": "Trending",
+    "more": "More"
   },
```

```diff
   "nav": {
     ...
     "userMenu": "账号菜单",
     "comingSoon": "敬请期待",
-    "browse": "浏览"
+    "browse": "浏览",
+    "trending": "热榜",
+    "more": "更多"
   },
```

### 4. Tests

- No existing `TopBar.nuxt.test.ts`. Skip — visual changes covered by PR
  review.
- `app/components/layout/{Sidebar,LocaleSwitch,ThemeSwitch}.nuxt.test.ts`
  are unaffected; they don't import `TopBar`.

## Critical files

- `app/components/layout/TopBar.vue` — primary work: split items, add
  Trending link, add More popover, move Publish to right group, add
  `Upload` + `ChevronDown` imports.
- `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue` — single line: add `id` and
  `scroll-mt-16` on the section.
- `i18n/locales/en.json`, `i18n/locales/zh.json` — `nav.trending` +
  `nav.more` only.

## Reused primitives (not changed)

- `Popover`, `PopoverTrigger`, `PopoverContent` from `app/components/ui/`
  (same trio used by `ThemeSwitch.vue` and `SaveToCollectionPopover.vue`).
- `useLocalePath`, `useI18n`, `useRoute` (all already used in current
  `TopBar.vue`).
- Lucide icons via `lucide-vue-next` (already a project dep).

## Out of scope (intentionally)

- No new `DropdownMenu` shadcn-vue component (Popover suffices).
- No changes to the `Sidebar`, `HomeHero`, `HomeTabs`, `HomeTagRail`, or
  any extension/detail components.
- No mobile sheet/hamburger redesign.
- No new routes — `Trending` is just `/#discovery`.

## Verification

1. `bun run dev`. From `/en` (any non-home page, e.g. `/en/extensions`):
   - Click `Trending` — URL changes to `/en/#discovery`, page scrolls to
     the discovery section with the heading visible (not clipped under
     the sticky header).
   - Repeat from `/en/cli` and `/en/collections` to confirm cross-route
     behavior.
2. Click `More ▾`:
   - Popover opens below the trigger with two items: Slash Commands and
     Plugins, both linking to `/extensions?category=…`.
   - Navigating to either category closes the popover and lands on the
     filtered grid.
   - When already on a More category, the `More` trigger shows the same
     bold + bottom-rule active treatment as the primary nav items.
3. Hover and click the `Upload` icon in the right group:
   - Tooltip / `aria-label` reads "Publish" (zh: "发布").
   - Click navigates to `/{locale}/publish`. While on `/publish`, the icon
     renders in full ink (otherwise muted).
4. Resize <768px:
   - Trending / More / Collections / Publish-icon all disappear.
   - The `Browse` link from the previous pass remains visible.
5. Theme toggle through Ivory / Dark / Mono — Popover + icon read
   correctly in all three.
6. Keyboard: tab through TopBar — order is Logo → Browse (mobile only) →
   Trending → Skills → MCP Servers → More trigger → Collections →
   Publish icon → Theme → Locale → User. Popover opens on Enter/Space and
   closes on Escape (Reka UI default).
7. Repeat the same checks from `/zh` to confirm copy: 热榜 / 更多 / 发布.
8. `bun run lint && bun run typecheck && bun run test` all green.

## Commit shape (per CLAUDE.md §Workflow)

One feature branch (`refactor/topbar-restructure` or similar), expect ~2
commits:

- `feat(topbar): add Trending shortcut and collapse Slash/Plugins under More`
- `refactor(topbar): demote Publish to a right-side Upload icon`

Could also be a single `refactor(topbar): restructure nav into primary/
more/publish-icon` if the diff is tight. No agent-attribution trailer per
project convention.

lastPrompt: but now I only can see 1 line, 3 recommended skills on homepage

allowedPrompts.prompt: run lint, typecheck, tests, and the seed script for verification
commit changes with conventional commit messages

# Discovery section: skills-aware heading + populate Recommended tab

## Context

The home discovery section currently shows three tabs (Recommended /
Popular / New), all of which hard-code `category: "skills"` in their
fetch — so the grid is skills-only. Two mismatches result:

1. **Heading lies.** The headline reads `{count} extensions for your
   team.`, but the count is a *cross-category* total (skills + MCP +
   slash + plugins) while the grid below only renders skills. The user
   asked for the heading to say "skills" — and we should also filter the
   `totalData` fetch to `category=skills` so the number matches reality.
2. **Recommended is sparse.** It shows a single row of 3 cards. The
   filter is `eq(extensions.badge, "official")` (`shared/search/query.ts:58-60`),
   and only **2** catalog entries currently have `badge: "official"`:
   `sql-tuner` and `aws-iam-policy-helper`. The slice limit (12) and API
   page size (24) are not the bottleneck — content is. The other tabs
   (Popular, New) are unfiltered so they already fill several rows.

User decision (this session): flag more catalog skills as official so
Recommended fills out naturally, rather than redefining the tab or
bolting on a fallback.

## Plan

### 1. Heading copy + scope — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Filter `totalData` to skills, and update the i18n strings. The grid-data
fetch already filters to skills, so we just align the headline.

```diff
 const { data: totalData } = await useFetch("/api/internal/extensions", {
   key: "home-discovery-total",
+  query: { category: "skills" },
   default: () => ({
     items: [] as ExtensionListItem[],
     total: 0,
     filters: {},
   }),
 })
```

### 2. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} extensions for your team.",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI extensions for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} skills for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI skills for your team.",
```

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个扩展，为你的团队而备。",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 扩展，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个技能，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 技能，为你的团队而备。",
```

### 3. Promote 11 catalog skills to `badge: "official"` — `shared/data/catalog.ts`

Editorial picks chosen for: ≥4.5★, healthy downloads, and a balanced
spread across `funcCat` × `subCat` cells so Recommended reads as a real
curation rather than "popular sorted differently". Skips entries already
flagged `popular` (those represent a separate badge axis).

End state: **13 official skills** (current 2 + 11 new), enough to fill
~4 rows on a 3-col desktop grid (12 cards on screen) with one spare.

| slug | funcCat / subCat | dl | ★ | reason |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `sql-tuner` *(keep)* | workTask / softDev | 142.5k | 4.9 | already official |
| `aws-iam-policy-helper` *(keep)* | business / cloud | 102.3k | 4.8 | already official |
| `api-spec-helper` | workTask / systemDesign | 52.8k | 4.6 | OpenAPI/REST helper, broadly useful |
| `dockerfile-linter` | workTask / softDev | 89.3k | 4.7 | container hygiene, high signal |

## Approved Plan:
# Discovery section: skills-aware heading + populate Recommended tab

## Context

The home discovery section currently shows three tabs (Recommended /
Popular / New), all of which hard-code `category: "skills"` in their
fetch — so the grid is skills-only. Two mismatches result:

1. **Heading lies.** The headline reads `{count} extensions for your
   team.`, but the count is a *cross-category* total (skills + MCP +
   slash + plugins) while the grid below only renders skills. The user
   asked for the heading to say "skills" — and we should also filter the
   `totalData` fetch to `category=skills` so the number matches reality.
2. **Recommended is sparse.** It shows a single row of 3 cards. The
   filter is `eq(extensions.badge, "official")` (`shared/search/query.ts:58-60`),
   and only **2** catalog entries currently have `badge: "official"`:
   `sql-tuner` and `aws-iam-policy-helper`. The slice limit (12) and API
   page size (24) are not the bottleneck — content is. The other tabs
   (Popular, New) are unfiltered so they already fill several rows.

User decision (this session): flag more catalog skills as official so
Recommended fills out naturally, rather than redefining the tab or
bolting on a fallback.

## Plan

### 1. Heading copy + scope — `app/components/home/HomeDiscovery.vue`

Filter `totalData` to skills, and update the i18n strings. The grid-data
fetch already filters to skills, so we just align the headline.

```diff
 const { data: totalData } = await useFetch("/api/internal/extensions", {
   key: "home-discovery-total",
+  query: { category: "skills" },
   default: () => ({
     items: [] as ExtensionListItem[],
     total: 0,
     filters: {},
   }),
 })
```

### 2. i18n — `i18n/locales/{en,zh}.json`

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} extensions for your team.",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI extensions for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} skills for your team.",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI skills for your team.",
```

```diff
- "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个扩展，为你的团队而备。",
- "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 扩展，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeading": "{count} 个技能，为你的团队而备。",
+ "discoveryHeadingEmpty": "AI 技能，为你的团队而备。",
```

### 3. Promote 11 catalog skills to `badge: "official"` — `shared/data/catalog.ts`

Editorial picks chosen for: ≥4.5★, healthy downloads, and a balanced
spread across `funcCat` × `subCat` cells so Recommended reads as a real
curation rather than "popular sorted differently". Skips entries already
flagged `popular` (those represent a separate badge axis).

End state: **13 official skills** (current 2 + 11 new), enough to fill
~4 rows on a 3-col desktop grid (12 cards on screen) with one spare.

|---|---|---|---|---|

| `aws-iam-policy-helper` *(keep)* | business / cloud | 102.3k | 4.8 | already official |
| `api-spec-helper` | workTask / systemDesign | 52.8k | 4.6 | OpenAPI/REST helper, broadly useful |

| `readme-generator` | tools / docs | 76.3k | 4.7 | universal team doc |
| `regex-builder` | tools / data | 51.2k | 4.7 | high-utility data tool |
| `conventional-commit-helper` | tools / vcs | 89.1k | 4.7 | strong vcs default |
| `github-actions-helper` | tools / vcs | 61.8k | 4.7 | second vcs pick |
| `test-this` | workTask / testing | 56.2k | 4.7 | filler-zone but quality-fit |

content.content:
1	// Idempotent catalog seed.
2	//
3	// Safe to run on every deploy. Mirrors scripts/seed-mcp-landscape.ts in
4	// strategy: upserts a fixed inventory of extensions (id prefix `cat-`) plus
5	// the small set of supporting rows it needs (default org, tag vocabulary),
6	// then prunes any catalog-prefixed rows no longer in the inventory. Real
7	// user-published extensions (any non-`cat-` id) are never touched.
8	//
9	// Wired into the Vercel build via `vercel-build` so a freshly deployed
10	// environment carries a populated /extensions catalog.
11	
12	import { and, inArray, notInArray, sql } from "drizzle-orm"
13	import { drizzle, type PostgresJsDatabase } from "drizzle-orm/postgres-js"
14	import postgres from "postgres"
15	
16	import { CATALOG, type CatalogEntry } from "../shared/data/catalog"
17	import * as schema from "../shared/db/schema"
18	import {
19	  extensions,
20	  extensionTags,
21	  organizations,
22	  tags,
23	} from "../shared/db/schema"
24	import { TAG_LABELS } from "../shared/tags"
25	
26	type Db = PostgresJsDatabase<typeof schema>
27	
28	const SYSTEM_ORG_ID = "default"
29	const CATALOG_ID_PREFIX = "cat-"
30	
31	function extensionIdFor(entry: CatalogEntry): string {
32	  return `${CATALOG_ID_PREFIX}${entry.slug}`
33	}
34	
35	/**
36	 * Upserts the catalog into `extensions` + `extension_tags`. Re-runnable;
37	 * never destructive to user data. The whole body runs inside one
38	 * transaction so a mid-step failure rolls back cleanly — particularly
39	 * important for step 4, where a crash between the delete and the insert
40	 * would otherwise leave catalog extensions with no tag links.
41	 *
42	 *   1. Ensure SYSTEM_ORG_ID exists (the owner for catalog rows).
43	 *   2. Upsert every tag from TAG_LABELS so referenced ids resolve in prod.
44	 *   3. Upsert each catalog entry by extension id.
45	 *   4. Replace tag links for each upserted extension (delete-then-insert
46	 *      scoped by extensionId — surgical, only the catalog row is touched).
47	 *   5. Prune extensions with id LIKE 'cat-%' not in the current CATALOG.
48	 */
49	export async function seedCatalog(db: Db): Promise<void> {
50	  await db.transaction(async (tx) => {
51	    // 1. Owner org — same constant as seed-mcp-landscape; safe to upsert again.
52	    await tx
53	      .insert(organizations)
54	      .values({
55	        id: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
56	        slug: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
57	        name: "Default Organization",
58	        nameZh: "默认组织",
59	      })
60	      .onConflictDoNothing({ target: organizations.id })
61	
62	    // 2. Tag vocabulary. seed.ts seeds these in dev; in prod nothing else
63	    //    does, so we upsert here too. onConflictDoNothing keeps existing
64	    //    rows verbatim in case admin curation has edited a label by hand.
65	    const tagRows = Object.entries(TAG_LABELS).map(([id, label]) => ({
66	      id,
67	      labelEn: label.en,
68	      labelZh: label.zh,
69	    }))
70	    await tx.insert(tags).values(tagRows).onConflictDoNothing()
71	    console.log(`seed-catalog: ensured ${tagRows.length} tags`)
72	
73	    // 3. Extension upserts. Catalog rows are visibility=published with a
74	    //    fixed publishedAt (so re-runs don't shift sort order); ownerOrgId
75	    //    is the SYSTEM_ORG; publisherUserId is null (the schema allows it).
76	    //    deptId stays null — catalog rows are department-agnostic.
77	    const now = new Date()
78	    const extRows = CATALOG.map((entry) => ({
79	      id: extensionIdFor(entry),
80	      slug: entry.slug,
81	      category: entry.category,
82	      scope: entry.scope,
83	      funcCat: entry.funcCat,
84	      subCat: entry.subCat,
85	      l2: entry.l2,
86	      badge: entry.badge ?? null,
87	      publisherUserId: null,
88	      ownerOrgId: SYSTEM_ORG_ID,
89	      deptId: null,
90	      iconEmoji: entry.iconEmoji,
91	      iconColor: entry.iconColor,
92	      visibility: "published" as const,
93	      name: entry.name,
94	      nameZh: entry.nameZh,
95	      tagline: entry.tagline,
96	      taglineZh: entry.taglineZh,
97	      description: entry.description,
98	      descriptionZh: entry.descriptionZh,
99	      downloadsCount: entry.downloadsCount,
100	      starsAvg: entry.starsAvg,
101	      ratingsCount: Math.floor(entry.downloadsCount / 50),
102	      publishedAt: now,
103	    }))
104	
105	    await tx
106	      .insert(extensions)
107	      .values(extRows)
108	      .onConflictDoUpdate({
109	        target: extensions.id,
110	        set: {
111	          slug: sql`excluded.slug`,
112	          category: sql`excluded.category`,
113	          scope: sql`excluded.scope`,
114	          funcCat: sql`excluded.func_cat`,
115	          subCat: sql`excluded.sub_cat`,
116	          l2: sql`excluded.l2`,
117	          badge: sql`excluded.badge`,
118	          ownerOrgId: sql`excluded.owner_org_id`,
119	          iconEmoji: sql`excluded.icon_emoji`,
120	          iconColor: sql`excluded.icon_color`,
121	          visibility: sql`excluded.visibility`,
122	          name: sql`excluded.name`,
123	          nameZh: sql`excluded.name_zh`,
124	          tagline: sql`excluded.tagline`,
125	          taglineZh: sql`excluded.tagline_zh`,
126	          description: sql`excluded.description`,
127	          descriptionZh: sql`excluded.description_zh`,
128	          downloadsCount: sql`excluded.downloads_count`,
129	          starsAvg: sql`excluded.stars_avg`,
130	          ratingsCount: sql`excluded.ratings_count`,
131	          updatedAt: sql`now()`,
132	        },
133	      })
134	    console.log(`seed-catalog: upserted ${extRows.length} extensions`)
135	
136	    // 4. Tag links — replace per-extension to keep edits in sync with the
137	    //    data file. Scoped delete + insert is safe: it never touches other
138	    //    rows. The transaction makes the delete+insert atomic so the table
139	    //    is never observed mid-state.
140	    const catalogExtIds = extRows.map((r) => r.id)
141	    await tx
142	      .delete(extensionTags)
143	      .where(inArray(extensionTags.extensionId, catalogExtIds))
144	    const tagLinkRows = CATALOG.flatMap((entry) =>
145	      entry.tags.map((tagId) => ({
146	        extensionId: extensionIdFor(entry),
147	        tagId,
148	      })),
149	    )
150	    if (tagLinkRows.length > 0) {
151	      await tx
152	        .insert(extensionTags)
153	        .values(tagLinkRows)
154	        .onConflictDoNothing()
155	    }
156	    console.log(`seed-catalog: wired ${tagLinkRows.length} tag links`)
157	
158	    // 5. Prune catalog rows whose slug has been removed from CATALOG. Bound
159	    //    by the `cat-` id prefix so non-catalog extensions are never affected.
160	    const desiredIds = catalogExtIds
161	    const stale = await tx
162	      .delete(extensions)
163	      .where(
164	        and(
165	          sql`${extensions.id} LIKE ${`${CATALOG_ID_PREFIX}%`}`,
166	          desiredIds.length > 0
167	            ? notInArray(extensions.id, desiredIds)
168	            : sql`true`,
169	        ),
170	      )
171	      .returning({ id: extensions.id })
172	    if (stale.length > 0) {
173	      console.log(`seed-catalog: pruned ${stale.length} stale entries (${stale.map((s) => s.id).join(", ")})`)
174	    } else {
175	      console.log(`seed-catalog: no stale entries to prune`)
176	    }
177	  })
178	}
179	
180	// ─── Standalone entry-point: `bun scripts/seed-catalog.ts` ────────────────────
181	async function main() {
182	  const url = process.env.DATABASE_URL
183	  if (!url) {
184	    console.error("seed-catalog: DATABASE_URL is not set")
185	    process.exit(1)
186	  }
187	  const client = postgres(url)
188	  const db = drizzle(client, { schema, casing: "snake_case" })
189	
190	  console.log("seed-catalog: starting")
191	  await seedCatalog(db)
192	  console.log("seed-catalog: done")
193	  await client.end()
194	}
195	
196	const isEntry = import.meta.url === `file://${process.argv[1]}`
197	if (isEntry) {
198	  main()
199	    .then(() => process.exit(0))
200	    .catch((err) => {
201	      console.error("seed-catalog: failed")
202	      console.error(err)
203	      process.exit(1)
204	    })
205	}
206

lastPrompt: but now the homepage still shows 3 recommended skills, how to add more?

content.text:
Found it. The catalog file is the source for the **seed script** — it doesn't change the live DB until that script runs. The good news: the seed correctly upserts `badge` for existing rows (`scripts/seed-catalog.ts:117` sets `badge: sql\`excluded.badge\`` on conflict), so re-running it will flip the 10 entries from no-badge to `official`.

Until that runs, the DB still has the old badge values and the API still returns 3 official skills.

You have two ways forward:

1. **Local dev** — run it yourself in your terminal so you can see the output:
   ```
   ! bun run scripts/seed-catalog.ts
   ```
   (the `!` prefix lets it run with your env loaded; needs `DATABASE_URL` set)

2. **Vercel** — push the branch and deploy; `vercel-build` runs `seed-catalog.ts` automatically (`package.json:9-10`).

I can run option 1 for you if you'd like — it touches your dev database, so I want to confirm before doing it. Want me to?

message.content: do we need add or update some tests for this branch?

content.text:
Honest take: most of the branch is CSS/copy refinement that no test would meaningfully cover, but there's one cheap, high-value test worth adding — a **catalog invariant** asserting that ≥12 catalog skills carry `badge: "official"` spanning multiple `funcCat`s. The badge set is editorial intent that lives in plain TS, and a casual edit could silently drop the Recommended tab back to 3 cards.

The TopBar structural changes (Trending link, More dropdown, Publish icon) are real behavior, but there's no existing `TopBar.nuxt.test.ts` in the repo and the changes are mostly visible-on-load — writing tests now would be net-new infrastructure rather than maintaining an existing pattern. I'd skip.

So my recommendation: **one small test on `shared/data/catalog.ts`**, nothing else. Want me to add it?

lastPrompt: do we need add or update some tests for this branch?

git add shared/data/catalog.test.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(catalog): guard the official-skill curation count and spread

A casual edit to shared/data/catalog.ts could quietly remove a 'badge:
"official"' line and shrink the home Recommended tab back to a single
row of cards, without any visible failure during dev. Assert two invariants:

- at least 12 catalog skills carry `badge: "official"`,
- the official set spans at least 3 functional categories.

Either failing means an editorial regression worth a second look.
EOF
)"
